overview.objective: Add multi-tenancy with per-organization data isolation while keeping the change transparent to regular users
overview.current_auth: Neon Native Auth (@neondatabase/auth/react/ui) - NOT Stack Auth
overview.key_principle: Admins manage organizations; users are transparently assigned to their org

batch.name: Database Schema Migration
batch.description: Add organizations table, organization_members junction, and org_id to all relevant tables

verification_steps.step: Run migration on staging: psql $DATABASE_URL -f scripts/009_add_organizations.sql
Verify Attorneyshare org created: SELECT * FROM organizations WHERE slug = 'attorneyshare';
Verify existing data has org_id: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_executions WHERE organization_id IS NOT NULL;
Verify users assigned to Attorneyshare org: SELECT u.email, om.role, o.name FROM organization_members om JOIN dashboard_users u ON u.id = om.user_id JOIN organizations o ON o.id = om.organization_id;
Verify invites have org_id: SELECT * FROM invites;

batch.name: Session Context Helper
batch.description: Create getSessionContext() helper that returns user info including their organization

file.purpose: Centralized session context with organization info

verification_steps.step: Import and call getSessionContext() in an existing API route
Verify it returns correct user and org info
Test with different users to verify org isolation

batch.name: Query Layer Updates
batch.description: Update all lib/db.ts query functions to filter by organization_id

file.description: Add organization_id parameter to all query functions

function.name: getQueriesForOrg
function.purpose: Create a bound query helper for a specific org

verification_steps.step: Update one function (getExecutions) and test
Create test data in two different orgs
Verify queries return only data for the specified org
Update remaining functions
Run existing tests to ensure no regressions

batch.name: API Route Updates
batch.description: Update all API routes to use session context and org-filtered queries

pattern.description: Standard pattern for all API routes

route.change: Add org context, filter by organizationId
Verify execution belongs to user's org before returning
Verify artifact belongs to user's org before signing
Require org context for data ingestion (API key includes org)
route.note: This needs special handling - see Batch 5

security_check.description: Add ownership verification for single-resource endpoints

verification_steps.step: Update /api/executions route
Test with users from different orgs
Verify user A cannot see user B's org data
Update remaining routes
Test all endpoints

batch.name: Organization Management APIs
batch.description: Create API routes for admins to manage organizations and members

file.purpose: List user's orgs, create new org (system admin only)

verification_steps.step: Test GET /api/organizations returns user's orgs
Test POST /api/organizations creates org (admin only)
Test member management endpoints
Test invite flow for new users

batch.name: Admin UI for Organization Management
batch.description: Create admin-only UI for managing organizations and members

file.purpose: Admin page to list and manage organizations
file.pseudocode:
"use client" import { useState, useEffect } from "react" import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button" import { Input } from "@/components/ui/input" import { Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardContent } from "@/components/ui/card" import { Table, TableBody, TableCell, TableHead, TableHeader, TableRow } from "@/components/ui/table" import { Dialog, DialogContent, DialogHeader, DialogTitle, DialogTrigger } from "@/components/ui/dialog" import { Plus, Users, Settings } from "lucide-react" import Link from "next/link" interface Organization { id: number name: string slug: string created_at: string member_count?: number } export default function OrganizationsPage() { const [organizations, setOrganizations] = useState([]) const [isCreateOpen, setIsCreateOpen] = useState(false) const [newOrg, setNewOrg] = useState({ name: "", slug: "" }) useEffect(() => { fetchOrganizations() }, []) async function fetchOrganizations() { const res = await fetch("/api/admin/organizations") const data = await res.json() setOrganizations(data.organizations) } async function createOrganization() { const res = await fetch("/api/organizations", { method: "POST", headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body: JSON.stringify(newOrg) }) if (res.ok) { setIsCreateOpen(false) setNewOrg({ name: "", slug: "" }) fetchOrganizations() } } return (

Organizations

Create Organization
setNewOrg({ ...newOrg, name: e.target.value })} /> setNewOrg({ ...newOrg, slug: e.target.value.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9-]/g, "-") })} />
Name Slug Created Members Actions {organizations.map((org) => ( {org.name} {org.slug} {new Date(org.created_at).toLocaleDateString()} {org.member_count || "-"}
))}
) }

file.purpose: Manage members of a specific organization
file.pseudocode:
"use client" import { useState, useEffect } from "react" import { useParams } from "next/navigation" import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button" import { Input } from "@/components/ui/input" import { Select, SelectContent, SelectItem, SelectTrigger, SelectValue } from "@/components/ui/select" import { Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardContent } from "@/components/ui/card" import { Table, TableBody, TableCell, TableHead, TableHeader, TableRow } from "@/components/ui/table" import { Badge } from "@/components/ui/badge" import { UserPlus, Trash2 } from "lucide-react" interface Member { id: number user_id: number user_email: string role: "owner" | "admin" | "viewer" joined_at: string } export default function OrgMembersPage() { const params = useParams() const orgId = params.id const [members, setMembers] = useState([]) const [inviteEmail, setInviteEmail] = useState("") const [inviteRole, setInviteRole] = useState<"admin" | "viewer">("viewer") useEffect(() => { fetchMembers() }, [orgId]) async function fetchMembers() { const res = await fetch(`/api/organizations/${orgId}/members`) const data = await res.json() setMembers(data.members) } async function inviteMember() { const res = await fetch(`/api/organizations/${orgId}/members`, { method: "POST", headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body: JSON.stringify({ email: inviteEmail, role: inviteRole }) }) if (res.ok) { setInviteEmail("") fetchMembers() } } async function removeMember(userId: number) { if (!confirm("Remove this member?")) return await fetch(`/api/organizations/${orgId}/members/${userId}`, { method: "DELETE" }) fetchMembers() } async function updateRole(userId: number, newRole: string) { await fetch(`/api/organizations/${orgId}/members/${userId}`, { method: "PATCH", headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body: JSON.stringify({ role: newRole }) }) fetchMembers() } const roleColors = { owner: "bg-purple-500", admin: "bg-blue-500", viewer: "bg-gray-500" } return (

Organization Members

{/* Invite Form */} Invite Member
setInviteEmail(e.target.value)} className="flex-1" />
{/* Members Table */} Email Role Joined Actions {members.map((member) => ( {member.user_email} {member.role} {new Date(member.joined_at).toLocaleDateString()}
{member.role !== "owner" && ( <> )}
))}
) }

navigation_update.description: Add Organizations link to admin navigation
navigation_update.pseudocode: // In admin layout or navigation component, add: <Link href="/admin/organizations"> <Building className="h-4 w-4" /> Organizations </Link>

verification_steps.step: Access /admin/organizations as system admin
Create a new organization
Add members to the organization
Test role changes
Test member removal

batch.name: First-Login Organization Assignment
batch.description: Automatically assign new users to their organization on first login (from invite)
batch.note: The invites.org_id column is already added in Batch 1 migration (009_add_organizations.sql)

verification_steps.step: Create an invite with a specific org_id
Sign up with the invited email
Verify user is assigned to correct org
Verify user sees only their org's data

batch.name: Row-Level Security Policies (Optional)
batch.description: Add Neon RLS policies for database-level security (extra protection layer)
batch.note: This batch is optional but recommended for production. RLS provides database-level protection even if application code has bugs.

file.purpose: Enable RLS and create policies for org isolation

considerations.item: RLS requires Neon Auth's auth.email() function to work
May impact query performance - test thoroughly
Service accounts (CI/CD) need bypass or separate handling
Can be enabled incrementally (one table at a time)

verification_steps.step: Enable RLS on test_executions first
Test queries with authenticated user
Verify user cannot access other org's data
Test admin bypass works
Enable RLS on remaining tables

new_tables.table: organizations (id, name, slug, created_by, created_at, updated_at)

default_organization.name: Attorneyshare
default_organization.note: All existing data is assigned to this organization

key_functions.function: requireOrgAdmin() - Auth check for org admins
requireSystemAdmin() - Auth check for system admins
